---
title: "Companion models & implicit integration"
subtitle: "T4 · Phase T4.1 · `va-transient::integrator`"
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Status: code complete — ladder rung 3, formally passed.** Backward Euler and trapezoidal, both
via a companion-model reduction to the same Newton machinery `va-core` already has. The RC
transient formally passes against real QSPICE golden (2026-07-18,
`t6-integration/03-validation.qmd`, `error=2.260e-5` against a `1e-3` tolerance).
:::

## Goal

Integrate a circuit with charge-storage state (capacitors, `ddt`) forward in time, reusing
`va-core`'s existing nonlinear solver rather than building a second one.

## Where it fits

```
 &[&dyn ModelInstance] ──[integrator::run]──► Waveform{t, x}
                              │
                    each step: build a Companion, hand it + the circuit to Newton
```

## The idea

**A companion model turns "integrate in time" into "solve one more nonlinear DC-shaped
system."** Both implemented methods reduce a device's charge-channel contribution to the exact
same shape a resistive term already has: `residual(x) + coeff·charge(x) + offset = 0`. Backward
Euler approximates `dQ/dt ≈ (Q(x) − Q_prev)/h`; trapezoidal averages the current and previous
`dQ/dt` estimates. Either way, once `coeff`/`offset` are computed from history, the *rest* of the
step is an ordinary Newton solve — no separate time-stepping solver needed, no change to
`va-core` at all.

**Only genuinely dynamic rows get the trapezoidal history term.** The `residual(x) = -dQ/dt`
identity trapezoidal's derivation leans on only holds for a row some device's charge channel
actually stamps — a real state variable. An ordinary KCL node with no capacitor, or a branch
constraint row, is *algebraic*: it must satisfy `residual(x) = 0` at every solved time
regardless of history. `is_dynamic` (computed once, from which rows charge/dcharge actually
touch) gates trapezoidal's own history term per-row — found the hard way, by a caller-supplied
`x0` whose branch-current entry didn't satisfy its own source's constraint exactly, which
injected a spurious permanent history term into that row and corrupted every subsequent step.
Backward Euler needed no such gate: its own `offset` is naturally `0` for an algebraic row
(`q_prev[i] = 0` there), for free.

**The caller owns the initial condition — the engine itself has no bias toward `x=0`.**
`run`/`run_with_events` take `x0: Vec<f64>` as a plain argument; nothing in this module forces
it to be zero. `rc_transient_matches_analytic` deliberately starts the capacitor at `0` V (not
already at steady state) to exercise a real charging transient, while a *different* test's
`x0` sets the source node directly to its own DC value. `va-cli::solve_transient` — one layer up
— is the one that always chooses `x0 = vec![0.0; dim]` (its own stated limitation, no `.ic`/`UIC`
support, `t6-integration/02-cli.qmd`); that's a choice at the integration layer, not a
restriction of this engine.

## The code

```rust
struct Companion { coeff: f64, offset: Vec<f64> }

impl Companion {
    fn backward_euler(q_prev: &[f64], h: f64) -> Self {
        Companion { coeff: 1.0 / h, offset: q_prev.iter().map(|q| -q / h).collect() }
    }
    fn trapezoidal(q_prev: &[f64], r_prev: &[f64], h: f64, is_dynamic: &[bool]) -> Self {
        let coeff = 2.0 / h;
        let offset = q_prev.iter().zip(r_prev).zip(is_dynamic)
            .map(|((q, r), &dynamic)| if dynamic { r - coeff * q } else { 0.0 })
            .collect();
        Companion { coeff, offset }
    }
}

pub fn run(
    instances: &[&dyn ModelInstance],
    dim: usize,
    x0: Vec<f64>,
    cfg: TranConfig,
) -> Result<Waveform, TransientError>;
```

`Waveform { t: Vec<f64>, x: Vec<Vec<f64>> }` — note `x[0]` is always literally the caller's `x0`,
not a solved sample (`t4-transient/02-lte-timestep.qmd`'s adaptive controller is what actually
grows `t`/`x` past that first entry).

## It works

```console
$ cargo test -p va-transient rc_transient
test integrator::tests::rc_transient_matches_analytic ... ok
```

Checked directly against the closed-form `V(t) = Vs·(1 − e^{−t/RC})`, not just "the tool agrees
with itself" — the same discipline `t3-core/02-newton.qmd`'s divider test follows. Driven through
the real netlist pipeline (`circuits/rc_step.net`, `t6-integration/{01-netlist,02-cli}.qmd`) and
compared against genuine QSPICE output, this is ladder rung 3:

```console
$ cargo run -q -p xtask -- validate
[xtask]   PASS circuits/rc_step.net: error=2.260e-5 (tol 1e-3)
```

Getting that QSPICE comparison right needed two more fixes on the harness side, both found
empirically — see `t6-integration/03-validation.qmd`'s "Two SPICE gotchas" section for the full
account (QSPICE solves the DC operating point before a `.tran` run by default; this engine's own
`x[0]` being the literal, unsolved seed misrepresented an algebraically-forced node).

## Limitations

`Method::Gear` is declared but not implemented (`TransientError::UnsupportedMethod`, never a
silent fallback to another method). The LTE estimate itself — the mechanism that decides *when*
a step is accepted — is its own phase, `t4-transient/02-lte-timestep.qmd`.

## What's next

`t4-transient/02-lte-timestep.qmd` — the adaptive step-size controller that decides how big `h`
gets to be at each step.
